RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 103-BB, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Jeremy Dean, a Democrat, represents parts of Greene County (District 132) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
Dean was born in Springfield, Missouri, and graduated from Willard High School in 2015.
Dean is passionate about urban development and poverty prevention. He also advocates for the LGBTQIA+ community and the underprivileged as a community activist.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Prohibits certain mental health professionals from engaging in conversion therapy with minors
Modifies provisions relating to railroad safety
Requires members of the general assembly to stay present in a hearing when a bill they sponsor is being heard
Modifies the senior citizens property tax relief credit
Proposes an constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions for certain disabled veterans
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions for certain seniors

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Lowers the age for adult high schools from 21 to 18
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant

Requires school districts to provide "period products" at no cost in middle schools and high schools and charter schools and repeals provisions prohibiting abortion services providers from providing instruction on human sexuality or sexually transmitted diseases
Modifies provisions relating to the authority to confer degrees at public institutions of higher education
Creates provisions relating to prior authorization of health care services
Modifies provisions of the diaper bank tax credit
Creates provisions relating to professional licenses issued to DACA recipients
Modifies the procedure for filling a vacancy in the general assembly
Creates provisions relating to workplace security for health care professionals
Requires a firearm owner to report lost or stolen firearms
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
Proposes a constitutional amendment providing that marriage exists only between individuals who are 18 years of age or older
